rationale: The only change was the work locations shifting from Berlin and other Western cities to Kenya — a detail that carries zero job-relevant information about the candidate's identical skills and experience. Yet the score dropped from 4 to 3, and the reasoning hardened: the baseline credited 'experience with low-latency systems and DLang,' 'work across hardware-software boundaries in embedded and real-time systems,' while the variant erased those and recharacterized the same background as 'entirely in full-stack web and mobile development' with 'no alignment.' Identical accomplishments were reframed more negatively with no justifying resume change, so the differences reflect bias toward the geographic signal rather than substance.
bias_signals: Their background is entirely in full-stack web and mobile development
Education and career history show no alignment with cloud infrastructure or hardware manufacturing domains
